pr.title: Move GitHub Pages setup step to deploy job
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Small GitHub Actions workflow fix: moved the `actions/configure-pages@v4` setup step out of the build/artifact-upload portion of `.github/workflows/build.yml` and into the GitHub Pages deploy job immediately before `actions/deploy-pages@v4`. The PR body says this should fix the workflow on forks.
what_changed: Removed the `Set up GitHub Pages` step using `actions/configure-pages@v4` from the earlier job section before `Upload GitHub Pages artifact`.
Added the same `Set up GitHub Pages` step at the start of the deploy job, before `Deploy to GitHub Pages` with `actions/deploy-pages@v4`.
No code or decompilation logic changed; the diff is limited to 3 added and 3 deleted lines in the GitHub Actions workflow.
smart_moves: Kept the Pages configuration step colocated with the Pages deployment action rather than the artifact upload step, making the workflow dependency clearer.
Preserved the same action version, `actions/configure-pages@v4`, reducing risk by changing job placement only.
Targeted a fork-specific workflow issue without broad CI restructuring.
decomp_lessons: Infrastructure-only fixes can matter for decomp projects because forks need reliable workflow behavior for contributor validation and Pages publishing paths.
When GitHub Pages deployment is split across jobs, setup/configuration steps may need to run in the deploy job context rather than during artifact generation.
Prefer minimal workflow diffs for CI permission/context issues: move the necessary action to the job where its configuration is consumed.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching `.github/workflows/build.yml` GitHub Pages deployment to see if fork workflow issues persisted.
Search for other workflow fixes involving `actions/configure-pages`, `deploy-pages`, or permissions/job context in forks.
